design_philosophy.core_loop: Drive down procedurally-presented freeway, destroy enemy vehicles with limited weapons, protect civilian traffic, earn score multiplier while on-road to unlock extra lives at thresholds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (ammunition, lives) against endless vehicle spawning; risk-reward of engaging enemies vs. maintaining safe driving line
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Spy operative in advanced armed vehicle conducting covert highway operations inspired by James Bond espionage fantasy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Penalizes player aggression—destroying civilian vehicles explicitly halts score counter, making discrimination between targets mechanically enforced. Game has no winning condition, only degradation; score-based progression creates tension between safety and advancement. Vertical scrolling driving genre is itself an opinionated constraint.

difficulty.lives_system: Player begins with unlimited cars during lead-in period, then must earn additional cars by reaching score thresholds; cars destroyed by collisions, enemy fire, or driving off-road
difficulty.checkpointing: None; endless procedural level with periodic terrain/weather shifts at road forks; no save/load
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Gear shifting (low/high) and weapon selection provide tactical control; road forks offer navigation choice; weapons van summoning button allows proactive loadout management

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary special weapons acquired via weapons van: oil slicks, smoke screens, surface-to-air missiles; each has limited ammo and persists until car destruction. Speedboat conversion sequences act as temporary vehicle upgrades.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Extra lives unlocked by reaching score thresholds; new regions/terrain unlocked at road forks (procedurally presented, not earned)
replayability_hooks: Endless mode with no victory condition encourages score-chasing competition
Procedural terrain and weather variation at forks creates session variety
Multiple weapon types reward experimenting with different tactical loadouts
Speedboat sequences break monotony of car-only driving

world_building_techniques: Vehicle design (G-6155 Interceptor sports car) evokes spy-tech fantasy
Enemy vehicle archetypes (helicopters dropping bombs, attacking boats) establish implicit hostile environment
Civilian traffic presence frames player as protector within militarized space
Terrain transitions (road → water → new regions) suggest expanding operational theater
Peter Gunn theme (James Bond-inspired music) reinforces spy narrative without explicit storytelling

danger_signals: Helicopter overhead dropping bomb craters into road (air superiority threat)
Enemy vehicles attacking from front and behind (encirclement tactic)
Score counter halt on civilian destruction (moral penalty mechanic)
environmental_storytelling: Road forks imply expanding territory requiring reconnaissance
Boathouse conversion points suggest pre-established operational infrastructure
Weapons van appearance suggests supply line logistics
Freeway setting frames conflict as domestic/infiltration scenario
